Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. C64: Putting Sprite Multiplexing to Work 37 upvotes, 1 comments
  2. The Xkcd thing, now interactive 643 upvotes, 82 comments
  3. First in-utero stem cell therapy for fetal spina bifida repair is safe: study 335 upvotes, 64 comments
  4. How to sew a Hyperbolic Blanket (2021) 36 upvotes, 3 comments
  5. Physicists developing a quantum computer that’s entirely open source 166 upvotes, 32 comments
  6. Show HN: I built a sub-500ms latency voice agent from scratch 502 upvotes, 149 comments
  7. Simple screw counter 212 upvotes, 58 comments
  8. Show HN: React-Kino – Cinematic scroll storytelling for React (1KB core) 10 upvotes, 0 comments
  9. DOS Memory Management 89 upvotes, 24 comments
  10. I built a pint-sized Macintosh 71 upvotes, 19 comments
  11. Claude's Cycles: Claude Opus 4.6 solves a problem posed by Don Knuth [pdf] 107 upvotes, 36 comments
  12. I'm reluctant to verify my identity or age for any online services 207 upvotes, 83 comments
  13. British Columbia is permanently adopting daylight time 1001 upvotes, 486 comments
  14. India's top court angry after junior judge cites fake AI-generated orders 233 upvotes, 103 comments
  15. We Built a Video Rendering Engine by Lying to the Browser About What Time It Is 109 upvotes, 46 comments
  16. Meta’s AI smart glasses and data privacy concerns 1264 upvotes, 720 comments
  17. Arm's Cortex X925: Reaching Desktop Performance 183 upvotes, 94 comments
  18. I'm losing the SEO battle for my own open source project 172 upvotes, 90 comments
  19. Mullvad VPN: Banned TV Ad in the Streets of London [video] 191 upvotes, 104 comments
  20. Launch HN: Cekura (YC F24) – Testing and monitoring for voice and chat AI agents 14 upvotes, 3 comments
  21. Ars Technica fires reporter after AI controversy involving fabricated quotes 474 upvotes, 290 comments
  22. Computer Says No 47 upvotes, 20 comments
  23. Apple introduces the new MacBook Air with M5 98 upvotes, 63 comments
  24. The Internet's Top Tech Publications Lost 58% of Their Google Traffic Since 2024 84 upvotes, 55 comments
  25. Florida public universities to pause hiring new H-1B workers 16 upvotes, 6 comments
  26. AI-generated art can't be copyrighted (Supreme Court declines review) 63 upvotes, 47 comments
  27. Don't Become an Engineering Manager 55 upvotes, 40 comments
  28. Apple unveils new Studio Display and all-new Studio Display XDR 93 upvotes, 75 comments
  29. History of the Graphical User Interface: The Rise (and Fall?) Of WIMP Design 18 upvotes, 11 comments
  30. Apple Introduces MacBook Pro with All‑New M5 Pro and M5 Max 255 upvotes, 280 comments

Last generated 12:00am, 4 March 2026. Source here.
